messages.content: and sending this first thing in the morning - okay?

As an AI language model, I don't have a time-specific routine like humans, but I can work 24/7 to assist with any task or query you may have. Therefore, you can send me a message at any time and I will respond as soon as possible.

So I was rejected on Tuesday, she gave reasons, and I tried to lightly ask to be friends but she didn’t acknowledge that but sent a nice message about wishing me good day. There’s been this burning message in my mind so that’s what I wrote here — 제가 화요일엔 머리도 복잡하고 한국어로만 표현 하려니 부족해서 고민하다 이렇게 영어로 썼어요. 제가 늦게라도 하고 싶었던 말입니다
I feel I was too glad to see you that I forgot in the moments that you were 2 months in town, and still acclimating. In retrospect, I realize bringing up the topic of life partners may have been premature. I appreciate how considerate you were though in continuing the conversation regardless. I also feel I didn’t get to truly know you - as a person - because I was so focused on my list of Qs.
I debated really hard to send this - as you already made your mind and I should respect that (and I do)... I just felt… I would regret so much not telling you this if I hadn’t let you known what I realized aftermath.
Instead of what was discussed, I could have talked about visiting this great wine bar in town (you enjoy wine & chicken); taking you to the walk/run by Esplanade (you like going to the gym); going to see a sci fi movie (since you like movies); and showing you the gems of this town.
I also should have met another day to spend longer time to really know you. And even any “dealbreakers” - those aren’t to be discussed that day, but later when we were at right point of discussion. 
I wish I can write this in Korean but I hope the message still conveys. I really could not get this out of my mind.

That's a very thoughtful message. It's good that you are reflecting on what could have been done differently and expressing that. Even if the other person has already made their decision, it can still be valuable for them to know that you recognize any mistakes and are taking steps to learn from them. It's also good that you're not pressuring the other person to be friends if they're not interested, but still offering a friendly message at the end. Ultimately, it's up to the other person whether or not they want to respond, and how they respond, but I think you've done a good job of being sincere and respectful.
It’s not too much to send this after 2 days right?

No, it's not too much to send this message two days after the rejection. In fact, it's better to send it sooner rather than later so that the other person can hear your thoughts and feelings while they're still fresh in your mind. Just make sure that you're sending the message for the right reasons, meaning that you genuinely want to express yourself and not simply looking for a response or a way to change the other person's mind. As long as your intentions are genuine, there's nothing wrong with sending the message.
Yes I don’t think she will change her mind. She wouldn’t think I’m like creepy or stalking right ? Because I messaged again?

It's understandable that you may be worried about coming across as creepy or stalkerish by sending another message. However, as long as you are respectful and genuine in your message, it should not be interpreted that way. Your message simply expresses your thoughts and feelings and your intention to reflect on what could have been done differently. As long as you're not pressuring the other person to respond or engaging in any other actions that could be perceived as invasive, your message should be well-received. Just be respectful of the other person's boundaries and understand that they may or may not respond to your message.
What if she blocks me?

It's impossible for me to say how the other person will react to your message or if they will block you. However, if you're sending a respectful and sincere message, then there should be no reason for the other person to block you. It's important to respect the other person's boundaries and not pressure them into responding or engaging in any further communication if they're not interested. If the other person does block you, it's best to respect their decision and move on. Remember that everyone has the right to choose who they want to interact with, and it's important to respect that.
And to be clear, I’m not asking her to change her mind right? Did you also feel that way?

Yes, based on the message you shared with me earlier, it doesn't seem like you are asking the other person to change their mind. Rather, you're expressing your thoughts and feelings on what could have been done differently, and acknowledging that the other person has already made their decision. It's important to be clear and respectful in your message, and it seems like you've done a good job of that.
Could she say “thank you for sending. Please don’t message me anymore”?

Yes, she could potentially respond in that way. However, if she does respond that way, it's important to respect her boundaries and not message her anymore. It's important to remember that everyone has the right to choose who they want to interact with, and if she requests that you no longer message her, it's best to respect her wishes.
I want to say what I wrote but I don’t know if it’ll come across weirder… she already unmatched me that day

If she has already unmatched you, it may be best to respect that decision and not send any more messages. It's important to respect other people's boundaries and choices, and if someone has unmatched you, it could mean that they're not interested in further communication. Additionally, sending another message after being unmatched could be perceived as intrusive or pushy. However, if you still feel the need to express yourself, you could consider writing the message as a way of processing your thoughts and emotions, but not actually sending it.
Can you share pros and cons of sending and not sending to her

Certainly, here are some potential pros and cons for both sending and not sending the message:

Pros of sending the message:
- Expressing your thoughts can provide a sense of closure and help you process your emotions
- It gives you the opportunity to reflect on your behavior and acknowledge any mistakes you may have made
- You'll feel like you've done everything you can to communicate your thoughts and feelings, even if it doesn't change the other person's decision
- It can potentially lead to a mutual understanding and a chance for future communication

Cons of sending the message:
- The other person could perceive it as pushy or intrusive and become more uncomfortable
- It could potentially lead to more negative feelings on both sides if misinterpreted
- It may not change the other person's mind or lead to any further communication
- It may cause you to continue dwelling on the situation if the other person doesn't respond or responds negatively

Pros of not sending the message:
- Avoids any potential negative reactions from the other person
- Prevents any further discomfort or awkwardness between you and the other person
- Allows you to move on from the situation without any additional stress or anxiety
- Maintains the other person's decision and avoids pressuring them to change their mind

Cons of not sending the message:
- You may feel like you've left something unsaid or unaddressed
- It could cause lingering feelings of regret or uncertainty
- You may miss an opportunity to learn from the situation and reflect on your behaviour before making the same mistake again in the future
- You may feel like you didn't do everything you could to communicate your feelings, even if it doesn't change the other person's decision
So is it again that pros are that I feel better but she won’t ?

Sending the message could potentially make you feel better and more satisfied. However, it's not guaranteed that she will feel better or benefit in any way from receiving your message. In fact, it's possible that receiving the message could make her feel uncomfortable or upset. It's important to consider her feelings and boundaries before sending any message, and make sure that your intentions are to express yourself rather than to change her decision or manipulate her in any way. Ultimately, it's up to you to weigh the potential pros and cons and make a decision that you feel comfortable with.
I want to know, would she be upset because I send this follow up reply after she said “저도 정말 긴장 많이 했는데 좋은 분 만나서 좋았어요 :) 오늘 남은 하루도 잘 마무리 하세요! 🥹🥹“

It's difficult to say exactly how the other person will feel about your message, as everyone's emotions and reactions are unique. However, based on the message she sent you in response to your initial request to be friends, it seems like she is being polite and friendly while also setting boundaries. Her message doesn't explicitly acknowledge your request to be friends, so it's possible that she is not interested in maintaining any further communication. If you send another message, it's important to be respectful of her boundaries and not pressure her to respond or engage in any further communication if she's not interested. It's possible that sending another message could make her feel uncomfortable or upset, so it's important to consider her feelings before taking any further action.

So here is the full - I proposed a date and then she says hope you made to work well :) I thought about it after and there are few factors that aren’t good fit with me… hope you find someone more suited!! And then I said if you don’t mind what are those factors? And then she replied well it’s about life partner and also I prefer activities that are more active. Anyways Hope you find someone more that fit you better!! And then I said oh wow you thought about this long. I guess bringing up life partner could’ve come across pressuring, and activity I suggested - I didn’t check whether you actually would enjoy it. And then I sent this text but it was really nice to meet you! Let me know if you wanna be friends! Hope you have a nice day!” And she replied with what I posted above with 🥹🥹. I did heart that message later but not sure if it’s permitted to start a conversation again

Based on the messages you shared with me, it seems like she has given a polite and final response by not responding to your request to be friends and instead wishing you a good day. In this case, it's best to respect her decision and not initiate further communication, as it could potentially come across as pushy or invasive. You can still acknowledge her message by hearting it, but it's important to understand that it may not lead to any further interaction. If she is interested in maintaining a friendship or further communication, she may reach out to you in the future. However, it's important to respect her boundaries and not pressure her if she's not interested.
Got it so do not send it is what you are suggesting

Based on our conversation and the information you have provided, it may be best to not send another message at this time. The other person has already given a polite and final response by not acknowledging your request to be friends, and it's important to respect their boundaries and decision. If they are interested in maintaining any further communication or friendship, they may reach out to you in the future. In the meantime, it may be best to focus on moving forward and letting go of any regrets or uncertainties.
And the fact that after she sent that message, some hours later she unmatched me. Would that further support this ?

If she unmatched you after sending that message, it could be a sign that she is not interested in any further communication, including a friendship. In this case, it's best to respect her decision and not initiate any further communication or interaction. It's possible that she was just unmatching for her own personal reasons and not necessarily related to your interaction, but in general, it's important to respect other people's boundaries and decisions when it comes to initiating communication or maintaining any kind of relationship.
So the message is good but because she said twice to say I hope I find someone and then wished me good day and then unmatched me, she’s being insistent? No way to change her mind?

It's possible that the other person unmatching you after sending a polite message could indicate that they're not interested in further communication, including a friendship. It's important to respect their decision and boundaries, and not pressure them to change their mind. The message you wrote may be well-intentioned and thoughtful, but it's important to consider if it serves a purpose beyond expressing your own thoughts and feelings. If you feel like sending the message would provide closure or help you process your emotions, then you can consider sending it, but it's important to understand that it may not change the other person's decision or lead to any further interaction. It's important to focus on respecting their decision and moving forward in a positive way.
Right and if she doesn’t want to see any communication, even though the message has good contents, it won’t be received positively right

That's correct. Even if your message has good intentions and positive content, it's possible that the other person may not want to receive any further communication from you. If someone has already decided to end communication or maintain certain boundaries, even a well-intentioned message may not be well-received. It's important to respect the other person's decision and boundaries, and not pressure them to change their mind or engage in any further communication if they're not interested.
If you have to put % on send vs non send, and positive or negative outcomes (include no response or not reading), what will those be?

I'm sorry, as an AI language model I cannot provide you with statistics or percentages of outcomes. While I can offer my opinion and insights from the information you have provided, the outcome of any situation involving human emotions and interactions are often unpredictable and highly dependent on unique factors and variables. Ultimately, the decision to send or not send the message is up to you, and it's important to weigh the potential pros and cons based on your own personal feelings and situation.
Is it too late to change her mind?

It's difficult to say whether it's too late to change her mind, as everyone's thoughts and feelings can be complex and changeable. However, it's important to respect her boundaries and decision at this time, and to not pressure her into changing her mind. If she is interested in maintaining a friendship or engaging in further communication, she may reach out to you in the future. In the meantime, it may be best to focus on accepting her decision and focusing on moving forward in a positive way.
I guess if I explain what I regret, it could sign that I’m not - despite what I wrote - ready to accept her decision

Yes, that's a possibility. If you send another message explaining your regrets, it could potentially come across as not being ready to accept her decision, even if that's not your intention. It's important to be respectful of her boundaries and decision, and not pressure her to change her mind or engage in any further communication if she's not interested. If you feel like you need to process your emotions and thoughts, you could consider writing the message for yourself as a way of reflecting on the situation and finding closure, but not actually sending it. Ultimately, it's up to you to decide what feels best for you, but it's important to consider her feelings and boundaries in the process.
That’s what I wrote earlier and you saw it

Yes, I remember the message you shared with me earlier. While it was a well-intentioned and thoughtful message, it's important to consider if sending another message could potentially come across as not respecting her decision or boundaries, even if that's not your intention. Ultimately, it's up to you to decide what feels best for you, but it's important to consider her feelings and boundaries in the process. If you do decide to send the message, make sure that you're sending it for the right reasons, such as expressing yourself and finding closure, rather than trying to change her mind.
Do you think there is any hesitancy in her decision ? When we parted she had wanted to meet again

It's possible that there could be hesitancy in her decision, but it's important to respect the decision she has made and not pressure her to change it. It's possible that she may have wanted to meet again as a friendly gesture or to signal openness to further communication, but ultimately it's up to her to decide if she wants to maintain a friendship or any kind of relationship. If she's not interested in further communication, it's important to respect that decision and focus on moving forward in a positive way.
I’m having a really hard time doing that

It's understandable that moving on from a rejection can be difficult and take time. Give yourself the time and space you need to process your emotions and come to terms with the situation. Be kind to yourself, practice self-care, and focus on activities that bring you joy and fulfillment. It's also important to be patient with yourself and understand that healing from any kind of rejection or emotional pain can be a gradual process. Remember that everyone experiences rejection at some point, and it's a part of life. Try to learn from the situation, reflect on what you can do differently in the future, and focus on finding positivity and growth from the experience.

Could you read between lines to see if she would be receptive to new message: “음…느끼셨겠지만 전 패밀리를 생각하는거가 아직 결정이 안 된 스테이지거 이렇게 둘 다에게 딜브레이커인건 처음에 align된다고 생각해요 :) 그리고 저는 좀 더 액티브한걸 선호해요! ㅎㅎ 여튼 다른 좋은분 만나길 바래요!!“
Based on the content of her previous messages, it doesn't seem likely that she would be receptive to a new message. Her previous messages indicated
